current_revision.content:
EO makes two claims that appear to pull in opposite directions. The first: the Ground/Figure/Pattern trichotomy is "applied without requiring a perceiver" — a bacterium performing SEN on a sugar gradient has no consciousness, but the operation has triadic structure. The second: every operator expression is "a perception at a time-bound point: subjective, perspectival, always revisable." If the structure is observer-independent, how is every instance of it perspectival?

The Quantum Parallel

Quantum mechanics has the same tension and has been sitting with it longer.

In QM, "observation" does not require a conscious observer. It requires interaction with another system. A photon hitting a detector is not being perceived. It is decohering through contact with a system that has different degrees of freedom. The measurement problem is not about minds. It is about what happens when systems with incompatible bases interact.

The critical insight: which basis you measure in determines which eigenvalues you get. A spin-½ particle measured along the z-axis yields spin-up or spin-down. The same particle measured along the x-axis yields different eigenvalues. The particle does not have a z-spin that the x-measurement fails to find. The interaction basis partially determines which decomposition is realized.

Interaction, Not Observation

Mapped onto EO: the trichotomy does not require a perceiver. It requires an interaction. Any system encountering another system produces an operator expression with Ground/Figure/Pattern structure, regardless of whether anything conscious is involved. In this sense the structure is ontological — it holds at the level of bacteria, thermodynamics, and molecular signaling.

But any particular operator expression is always from somewhere. It is one system interacting with another, and the interaction basis determines what shows up. This is the sense in which the structure is perspectival — not because a mind is required, but because there is no basis-free interaction.

Measurement Collapse in EO

A particular interaction basis collapses phenomena into particular positions on the Site Face. The same phenomenon, encountered through different interaction bases, lands in different cells.

An institution measuring homelessness through intake forms collapses the client into Entity (Existence × Figure) — a record with fields. The caseworker interacting with the same person face to face may register Field (Structure × Ground) — a relational environment, the web of informal supports that no dropdown captures. A policy analyst reviewing aggregate data may register Kind (Existence × Pattern) — a population category. Same person. Different interaction basis. Different Site cell.

None of these is wrong. None is complete. Each is a real decomposition produced by a real interaction. The question "which Site cell does this person actually occupy" has the same structure as "what is the particle's actual spin" — it presupposes a basis-free measurement that does not exist.

Relationship to the Experience Engine

The Experience Organ's Rule 4 — the Anti-Omniscience Axiom — formalizes this: there is no God's-eye view. All memory availability is mediated by horizon. In QM terms: there is no measurement that is not in some basis. Every query to the Experience Organ passes through a horizon (a perspectival context), and the horizon is the interaction basis that determines which decomposition is returned.

Rule 5 — Restrictivity — adds that horizons can only narrow access, never widen it beyond what the interaction basis permits. You cannot recover x-basis information from a z-basis measurement by wanting it more.

Rule 9 — Defeasibility — adds that no decomposition is globally immune to revision. A different interaction basis can always produce a different cut.

What This Resolves and What It Doesn't

Resolved: The apparent contradiction between "no perceiver required" and "always perspectival." The structure is interaction-dependent, not observer-dependent. Interactions have triadic structure whether or not consciousness is present. But every interaction is from somewhere, so every operator expression is perspectival. Both claims hold. They describe different levels — the structural level (the trichotomy holds for any interaction) and the instance level (any particular expression is basis-dependent).

Not resolved: Whether there is a deeper level at which the interaction bases themselves have triadic structure. QM does not know whether the measurement problem has a solution or is a permanent feature. EO does not know whether interaction-dependence is a limitation of the framework's descriptive reach or a structural feature of transformation itself. The framework maps expressible transformation. Whether there is inexpressible transformation that is not interaction-dependent is outside its scope — by design, but the design choice is not thereby justified.

Not resolved: Whether particular interaction bases systematically privilege certain Site cells. The cross-linguistic data shows that every human language underserves the Ground column (Void, Field, Atmosphere). If language is an interaction basis, this is a species-wide measurement bias — the equivalent of only having z-axis detectors. Whether non-linguistic interaction bases (embodied, relational, ecological) access the Ground column more readily is an empirical question that EO has not tested.

See Also

  • Ground, Figure, Pattern

  • The Three Faces

  • The Experience Engine

  • Bivalent Compression

  • √2: Pythagorean Origins and the Meno Proof

References

  • Zurek, W.H. (2003). "Decoherence, einselection, and the quantum origins of the classical." Reviews of Modern Physics, 75(3), 715–775.

  • Schlosshauer, M. (2005). "Decoherence, the measurement problem, and interpretations of quantum mechanics." Reviews of Modern Physics, 76(4), 1267–1305.
